data.title: Sprint mid-implementation gap detection and routing
data.description:
## Context
DSO practitioners running /dso:sprint have no structured recovery when implementation reveals the plan is wrong. Failed tasks silently revert to open and the sprint continues on a broken decomposition. There is no pre-flight check for codebase drift between planning and execution, no escalation when implementation agents lack confidence in task descriptions, no remediation path when story validation fails despite all tasks being closed, and no mechanism to incorporate out-of-scope review feedback into the plan. This epic adds a detection layer to sprint at four lifecycle checkpoints, routing plan-level gaps to implementation-plan for autonomous re-planning.

## Success Criteria
1. At sprint entry, sprint compares the git commit history since task ticket creation against the file impact table from the original implementation plan; if any file in the impact table was modified by an external commit, implementation-plan is autonomously re-invoked preserving closed tasks, and a REPLAN_TRIGGER: drift comment is recorded on the epic
2. Implementation sub-agents emit a structured confidence signal: CONFIDENT (proceed) or UNCERTAIN:<reason> (fail with explanation). When an agent emits UNCERTAIN twice on the same task within a single sprint invocation, sprint routes the task to implementation-plan for re-planning. Failure tracking is per story, not per task ID, to prevent counter resets on task replacement.
3. When all tasks for a story are closed but story-level validation fails, sprint invokes implementation-plan to create additional remediation tasks following standard TDD flow (RED test task first, then implementation task)
4. When code review requests modifications to files not listed in the original task file impact, the sprint orchestrator routes the finding to implementation-plan to create new tasks covering those files — the orchestrator chooses this path when the reviewer finding is accepted rather than defended. This escalation fires only between batches, not mid-batch.
5. The self-healing loop is observable: each trigger records a structured REPLAN_TRIGGER: <drift|failure|validation|review> comment on the epic, and each resolution records REPLAN_RESOLVED: <implementation-plan|brainstorm> — enabling post-delivery measurement of escape hatch usage and resolution tier

## Dependencies
Depends on f997-ed78 (Implementation-plan re-invocation safety) — sprint detection routes to implementation-plan which must safely handle re-invocation with in-flight ticket graphs.
Soft dependency on 0d66-ceb6 (Unified Primary Ticket Model for Sprint Skill) — sprint entry drift detection should coordinate to avoid rework on sprint entry logic.

## Approach
Sprint gains a detection layer at four lifecycle checkpoints: entry (drift check), post-failure (confidence signal routing), story validation (remediation task creation), and review (out-of-scope feedback routing). Sub-agents gain a CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN output signal. All triggers record structured REPLAN_TRIGGER comments for observability. Review escalation fires only between batches to avoid mid-batch task injection conflicts. Non-interactive mode uses INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED for brainstorm escalation.

## Scenario Analysis
- Mid-batch task injection from review escalation (high): Addressed by SC4 — fires only between batches
- Non-interactive CI/CD blocking on brainstorm confirmation (high): Uses INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED pattern from debug-everything
- Git drift false negative on transitive deps (high): Drift check covers direct impact table only (documented limitation)
- Failure counter reset on new task ID (high): Addressed by SC2 — tracking per story
- Post-brainstorm cascade may exceed 5 sub-agent limit (medium): Cascade executes between batches

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 9 (from parent epic analysis, filtered to this scope)
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
